ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

Our business is subject to a number of risks and uncertainties that could adversely affect our operating results, financial condition, and future prospects. The following is a discussion of material factors that make an investment in our securities risky or speculative.

RISKS RELATED TO OUR INTERNATIONAL OPERATIONS AND SUPPLY CHAIN

Tariff and Trade Policy Risks

We manufacture approximately 96% of our footwear and apparel products through independent contractors, primarily located in Vietnam, China, and Indonesia. Substantially all of our products are manufactured outside of the United States. Our business is therefore subject to risks associated with:

- Changes in trade policy and import/export regulations, including new or increased tariffs, import quotas, or other trade barriers
- Retaliatory trade measures imposed by other countries in response to U.S. trade policy
- Section 301 tariffs on goods imported from China, which currently apply to a portion of our product range

Our Greater China segment represented approximately 16% of our total revenues in fiscal year 2024. Approximately 28% of our footwear was manufactured in Vietnam during this period, and 18% in Indonesia.

If additional tariffs or other trade restrictions are imposed on products manufactured in these countries, including potential broad-based tariffs under any new trade legislation, our cost of goods sold could increase materially, and we may not be able to pass all such costs to consumers without adversely affecting demand.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS

Overview

Net revenues increased 2% to $51.4 billion in fiscal 2024. The impact of tariff headwinds on our gross margin was approximately 40 basis points, primarily from Section 301 tariffs applied to China-manufactured goods imported into the United States.
